questions.question: Bedrock foundation models are accessed through which pattern?
options: A single managed API for multiple providers
Direct EC2 SSH to model hosts
questions.explain: Amazon Bedrock provides managed API access to multiple model providers from one service endpoint.
whyWrong: SSH access to model hosts is not how Bedrock is consumed.
Notebook kernels are optional tooling, not the Bedrock access model.
CloudTrail is for auditing, not model inference transport.

questions.question: For semantic search in RAG, which model type is best?
questions.explain: Embeddings convert text into vectors used for similarity search in vector stores.
whyWrong: Image generation is unrelated to text similarity indexing.
Speech-to-text transcribes audio, not semantic vectorization.
Classification endpoints return labels, not retrieval vectors.

questions.question: Which Bedrock capability enforces topic restrictions and PII handling?
questions.explain: Guardrails define blocked topics, content filters, and sensitive information controls across supported models.
whyWrong: Knowledge Bases handle retrieval context, not safety policy.
Agents orchestrate tools and workflows, not core moderation policy.
Prompt caching is a latency/cost feature, not a policy control.

questions.question: You need end-to-end request latency tracing across Lambda and API Gateway. Use:
questions.explain: AWS X-Ray provides distributed tracing and service maps for latency bottleneck analysis.
whyWrong: CloudTrail Lake is for audit query analytics.
Access Analyzer is for policy exposure findings.
Trusted Advisor gives best-practice checks, not traces.

questions.question: Which service extracts tables/forms/text from PDFs for preprocessing?
questions.explain: Amazon Textract is purpose-built for OCR and structured document extraction including forms/tables.
whyWrong: Polly is text-to-speech.
Rekognition Custom Labels is for images/video object labeling.
Glue is ETL/catalog service, not OCR extraction.

questions.question: What is the best low-variance setting for deterministic output?
questions.explain: Lower temperature reduces randomness and improves consistency for policy/contract outputs.
whyWrong: Higher temperature increases creativity and variance.
Top-k alone does not replace temperature behavior control.
System prompts remain important for behavior constraints.

questions.question: Need IDE-native AWS coding assistant for SDK and infrastructure snippets:
questions.explain: Amazon Q Developer is integrated into IDEs to assist with code, AWS APIs, and development workflows.
whyWrong: Q Business is for enterprise knowledge chat over data sources.
Artifact provides compliance reports, not coding assistance.
Approval rules are repository governance features.

questions.question: For asynchronous decoupling of prompt enrichment and inference jobs, use:
questions.explain: Amazon SQS enables decoupled asynchronous processing between producer and consumer services.
whyWrong: WAF filters web requests; it is not a queue.
Route 53 health checks are DNS availability mechanisms.
Organizations handles account governance.

questions.question: Which storage is most common for immutable prompt/response archives with lifecycle rules?
questions.explain: Amazon S3 provides durable object storage with lifecycle policies and compliance-friendly archival patterns.
whyWrong: ElastiCache is in-memory and not for durable archives.
EBS snapshots are volume backups, not primary archive workflow.
Cloud9 local storage is ephemeral for dev environments.

questions.question: Best service to monitor custom app metrics and alarms for token usage?
questions.explain: CloudWatch custom metrics plus alarms are standard for runtime KPI monitoring and alerting.
whyWrong: Shield is DDoS protection.
Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.
Budgets tracks spend but not near-real-time app telemetry.

questions.question: Which identity service provides temporary AWS credentials for applications?
questions.explain: AWS STS issues temporary security credentials for federated and role-based access patterns.
whyWrong: RAM shares resources across accounts, not credentials.
IQ is consulting marketplace.
Access Advisor shows service-last-access info.

questions.question: Need private network access from VPC workloads to Bedrock APIs. Use:
questions.explain: Interface VPC endpoints/PrivateLink patterns provide private connectivity without traversing the public internet.
whyWrong: NAT still egresses to public endpoints.
S3 website endpoints are unrelated to Bedrock API access.
DNS firewall does not create transport connectivity.

questions.question: To build tool-using conversational workflows with action orchestration in Bedrock:
questions.explain: Agents orchestrate multi-step reasoning and invoke tools/APIs to fulfill user intents.
whyWrong: Knowledge Bases provide retrieval context but no broad orchestration.
Guardrails apply safety controls, not action orchestration.
StackSets deploy infrastructure and are unrelated.

questions.question: Best datastore for low-latency key-value session state in serverless quiz app:
questions.explain: DynamoDB is a fully managed low-latency NoSQL datastore ideal for session and metadata state.
whyWrong: Neptune is graph database for relationship-heavy queries.
Backup vault stores backups, not active session data.
Redshift is columnar data warehouse for analytics.

questions.question: Which service provides managed vector capabilities integrated with OpenSearch?
questions.explain: Amazon OpenSearch Service supports vector search for semantic retrieval use cases.
whyWrong: Glue crawler catalogs data; it does not serve vector similarity queries.
Firehose is ingestion delivery service.
CloudFront Functions run edge JS, not vector DB operations.

questions.question: To schedule periodic evaluation jobs without managing servers:
questions.explain: EventBridge Scheduler invokes targets on cron/rate schedules as a managed service.
whyWrong: EC2 loops add ops burden and poor reliability practices.
Manual CLI runs are not scalable operations.
Policy simulator is for IAM testing.

questions.question: For centralized API audit trail (who called what/when), primary service is:
questions.explain: CloudTrail records API events across AWS services for governance and investigations.
whyWrong: Config tracks resource configuration states/compliance.
RUM collects web app client metrics.
CodeArtifact is package repository service.

questions.question: Best way to deliver static SPA quiz app globally with HTTPS + CDN:
questions.explain: CloudFront with S3 static assets is standard for global low-latency static web delivery.
whyWrong: Single-region EC2 lacks CDN edge acceleration/resilience.
Route 53 DNS alone does not host app assets.
Batch is for compute job scheduling, not web hosting.

questions.question: Need secrets for external API keys with rotation support:
options: Lambda env vars committed to git
questions.explain: Secrets Manager securely stores secrets and supports automated rotation patterns.
whyWrong: Inventory tracks software metadata, not secrets.

Committing env values is insecure and noncompliant.

whyWrong: Neptune is graph database for relationship-heavy queries.
Backup vault stores backups, not active session data.
Redshift is columnar data warehouse for analytics.

questions.question: To build tool-using conversational workflows with action orchestration in Bedrock:
questions.explain: Agents orchestrate multi-step reasoning and invoke tools/APIs to fulfill user intents.
whyWrong: Knowledge Bases provide retrieval context but no broad orchestration.
Guardrails apply safety controls, not action orchestration.
StackSets deploy infrastructure and are unrelated.

questions.explain: DynamoDB is a fully managed low-latency NoSQL datastore ideal for session and metadata state.
whyWrong: Neptune is graph database for relationship-heavy queries.
Backup vault stores backups, not active session data.
Redshift is columnar data warehouse for analytics.

questions.question: Which service provides managed vector capabilities integrated with OpenSearch?
questions.explain: Amazon OpenSearch Service supports vector search for semantic retrieval use cases.
whyWrong: Glue crawler catalogs data; it does not serve vector similarity queries.
Firehose is ingestion delivery service.
CloudFront Functions run edge JS, not vector DB operations.

questions.question: To schedule periodic evaluation jobs without managing servers:
questions.explain: EventBridge Scheduler invokes targets on cron/rate schedules as a managed service.
whyWrong: EC2 loops add ops burden and poor reliability practices.
Manual CLI runs are not scalable operations.
Policy simulator is for IAM testing.

questions.question: For centralized API audit trail (who called what/when), primary service is:
questions.explain: CloudTrail records API events across AWS services for governance and investigations.
whyWrong: Config tracks resource configuration states/compliance.
RUM collects web app client metrics.
CodeArtifact is package repository service.

questions.question: Best way to deliver static SPA quiz app globally with HTTPS + CDN:
questions.explain: CloudFront with S3 static assets is standard for global low-latency static web delivery.
whyWrong: Single-region EC2 lacks CDN edge acceleration/resilience.
Route 53 DNS alone does not host app assets.
Batch is for compute job scheduling, not web hosting.

questions.question: Need secrets for external API keys with rotation support:
options: Lambda env vars committed to git
questions.explain: Secrets Manager securely stores secrets and supports automated rotation patterns.
whyWrong: Inventory tracks software metadata, not secrets.
Logs are not secure secret stores.
Committing env values is insecure and noncompliant.

questions.question: When grounding answers with enterprise documents in Bedrock, use:
questions.explain: Knowledge Bases manage ingestion, embeddings, retrieval, and augmentation flow for RAG.
whyWrong: Long prompts without retrieval are brittle and expensive.
CloudTrail Insights detects unusual API activity.
Athena query is analytics, not direct Bedrock retrieval orchestration.

questions.question: Bedrock foundation models are accessed through which pattern?
options: A single managed API for multiple providers
Direct EC2 SSH to model hosts
questions.explain: Amazon Bedrock provides managed API access to multiple model providers from one service endpoint.
whyWrong: SSH access to model hosts is not how Bedrock is consumed.
Notebook kernels are optional tooling, not the Bedrock access model.
CloudTrail is for auditing, not model inference transport.

questions.question: For semantic search in RAG, which model type is best?
questions.explain: Embeddings convert text into vectors used for similarity search in vector stores.
whyWrong: Image generation is unrelated to text similarity indexing.
Speech-to-text transcribes audio, not semantic vectorization.
Classification endpoints return labels, not retrieval vectors.

questions.question: Which Bedrock capability enforces topic restrictions and PII handling?
questions.explain: Guardrails define blocked topics, content filters, and sensitive information controls across supported models.
whyWrong: Knowledge Bases handle retrieval context, not safety policy.
Agents orchestrate tools and workflows, not core moderation policy.
Prompt caching is a latency/cost feature, not a policy control.

questions.question: You need end-to-end request latency tracing across Lambda and API Gateway. Use:
questions.explain: AWS X-Ray provides distributed tracing and service maps for latency bottleneck analysis.
whyWrong: CloudTrail Lake is for audit query analytics.
Access Analyzer is for policy exposure findings.
Trusted Advisor gives best-practice checks, not traces.

questions.question: Which service extracts tables/forms/text from PDFs for preprocessing?
questions.explain: Amazon Textract is purpose-built for OCR and structured document extraction including forms/tables.
whyWrong: Polly is text-to-speech.
Rekognition Custom Labels is for images/video object labeling.
Glue is ETL/catalog service, not OCR extraction.

questions.question: What is the best low-variance setting for deterministic output?
questions.explain: Lower temperature reduces randomness and improves consistency for policy/contract outputs.
whyWrong: Higher temperature increases creativity and variance.
Top-k alone does not replace temperature behavior control.
System prompts remain important for behavior constraints.

questions.question: Need IDE-native AWS coding assistant for SDK and infrastructure snippets:
questions.explain: Amazon Q Developer is integrated into IDEs to assist with code, AWS APIs, and development workflows.
whyWrong: Q Business is for enterprise knowledge chat over data sources.
Artifact provides compliance reports, not coding assistance.
Approval rules are repository governance features.

questions.question: For asynchronous decoupling of prompt enrichment and inference jobs, use:
questions.explain: Amazon SQS enables decoupled asynchronous processing between producer and consumer services.
whyWrong: WAF filters web requests; it is not a queue.
Route 53 health checks are DNS availability mechanisms.
Organizations handles account governance.

questions.question: Which storage is most common for immutable prompt/response archives with lifecycle rules?
questions.explain: Amazon S3 provides durable object storage with lifecycle policies and compliance-friendly archival patterns.
whyWrong: ElastiCache is in-memory and not for durable archives.
EBS snapshots are volume backups, not primary archive workflow.
Cloud9 local storage is ephemeral for dev environments.

questions.explain: CloudWatch custom metrics plus alarms are standard for runtime KPI monitoring and alerting.
whyWrong: Shield is DDoS protection.

Budgets tracks spend but not near-real-time app telemetry.

whyWrong: RAM shares resources across accounts, not credentials.
IQ is consulting marketplace.
Access Advisor shows service-last-access info.

DNS firewall does not create transport connectivity.
